$comment: D-LANG-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-INT-POINTEE-COMPAT (SQLite testfixture arc, TF-C41): RUN witness for the shipped-FFI-descriptor integer-pointee call-arg relaxation. tcl.json models `Tcl_GetWideIntFromObj(Tcl_Interp*, Tcl_Obj*, Tcl_WideInt*)` as `fn(..., ptr<i64>) -> i32` (an abstract width-based pointee). The testfixture calls it as `Tcl_GetWideIntFromObj(interp, obj, &iVal)` with `iVal` a Tcl_WideInt/sqlite3_int64 (typedef long long) -- pre-change DSS rejected the `long long*` arg S0003 (distinct IDENTITY, same REPRESENTATION on LP64; gcc accepts). The relaxation admits the same-representation integer pointer at the shipped-descriptor CALL-ARG boundary ONLY, and libtcl fills the caller's wide int. Here `Tcl_Eval("expr 20 + 22")` yields a wide-int result and `Tcl_GetWideIntFromObj(ip, res, &wide)` has libtcl write 42 into `wide` (sqlite3_int64* into ptr<i64>); the return IS that libtcl-filled value -- value-divergent. elf-x86_64 only (tcl.json availableObjectFormats:[elf]); CI ubuntu x86_64 runs it (libtcl8.6). RED-ON-DISABLE: set pointerConversions.ffiDescriptorIntPointeeCompat=false in c-subset.lang.json -> the &wide argument raises S0003 -> stops compiling.
